content:
💡 timelinize | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a private, self-hosted platform for archiving and visualizing all their digital data and memories in a chronological timeline.
✨ Key Features: • Data aggregation from diverse sources (photos, videos, chats, social media, location, contacts) • Self-hosted and user-owned data storage • Timeline visualization of imported data • 3D map view with historical location data
📖 Summary: Timelinize is a self-hosted application that aggregates data from various sources like photos, chats, social media, and location history into a single, cohesive timeline on your own computer. It allows for deep data import and organization, enabling users to own and preserve their digital memories forever.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 kill-ai-slop | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To help developers and designers identify and eliminate common, often unintentional, AI-generated design patterns and copy tics in their web projects, leading to cleaner, more human-like products.
✨ Key Features: • Multilingual field guide website cataloging 33 AI-slop tells • Interactive before→after demos for each tell • Agent Skill (`kill-ai-slop`) to scan projects for AI-generated code signals
📖 Summary: Kill AI Slop is a project that identifies and helps remove common visual and copy design 'tics' often found in AI-generated products. It offers a multilingual website cataloging these tells with interactive demos and an Agent Skill that scans codebases to detect and suggest fixes for these AI-generated patterns.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 pocket_disasm | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling coding agents and LLMs to perform parallel, headless binary analysis on multiple files simultaneously using IDA Pro's IDALib, managed through a unified MCP endpoint.
✨ Key Features: • Single MCP endpoint for multiple IDALib sessions • True multi-session analysis with independent IDALib processes • Parallel agent workflows for simultaneous binary analysis • IDA Pro MCP compatibility via pinned ida-pro-mcp • Agent-managed sessions for opening and switching binaries
📖 Summary: Pocket Disasm is a Python-based control layer for headless binary analysis using IDA Pro's MCP toolset. It allows coding agents to manage multiple, isolated IDALib sessions concurrently through a single HTTP endpoint, without requiring interactive IDA GUI instances.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 cursor-kit-for-ai | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Accelerating the development of AI-powered applications and services by providing pre-configured, production-ready project structures and AI agent skills.
✨ Key Features: • Production-ready project templates • CLI for project creation and plugin management • Extensible plugin system for Cursor AI skills • Support for various stacks including FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, and Kubernetes
📖 Summary: Cursor Kit for AI is a scaffolding library that provides production-ready project templates and plugins for developers using Cursor AI. It enables the rapid creation of full-stack applications, bots, and DevOps solutions with a single CLI command, significantly accelerating development.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 hh-ai-agent | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the job application process on HH.ru by leveraging AI for intelligent filtering and personalized cover letter generation.
✨ Key Features: • Automated job searching and application on HH.ru • Intelligent filtering of job postings using LLM • Personalized cover letter generation • Cloudflare and HH.ru anti-blocking measures • Telegram integration for notifications and HR message forwarding
📖 Summary: This Python repository automates the job application process on HH.ru by using an AI agent to find relevant job postings, filter out unsuitable ones, and generate personalized cover letters. It integrates with Telegram for notifications and HR responses, and uses a local LLM (Llama 3) for intelligent filtering and writing.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
